codeReview.summary: Well-implemented config management feature. The `werk config set` and `werk config get` commands correctly handle dot-notation keys, TOML persistence, global fallback, and malformed config errors. Code is clean, well-documented, and follows project conventions. All 34 tests pass (18 integration + 16 unit).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md architecture section lists a planned `editor.rs` module for $EDITOR integration, but this module does not yet exist. The current reality/desire commands appear to implement editor functionality inline in main.rs rather than via a dedicated module. Consider updating AGENTS.md to reflect the actual implementation pattern, or creating the editor.rs module as planned.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line 64 shows `editor.rs` in the architecture diagram, but no editor.rs file exists in werk-cli/src/. The reality/desire commands in main.rs implement editor logic directly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed rust-cli-worker skill procedure correctly. TDD approach was used (tests written first), all validation contract assertions covered, and proper error handling implemented. No deviations noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true with no deviations. Test file committed before implementation, all VAL-CFG assertions verified.

summary: The foundation-config feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. The code correctly handles: (1) dot-notation keys with flatten/unflatten, (2) TOML type preservation (string/int/bool/float), (3) global config fallback when no local workspace, (4) graceful malformed TOML errors via WerkError::ConfigError, (5) JSON output for both commands. Test coverage is comprehensive with 18 integration tests and 16 unit tests. All tests pass, clippy reports zero warnings, and no sd-core regressions detected. No blocking issues found.
